methodology: Pricing triangulated from PSD/GeBIZ tender awards (public), PLRD Grade A/B/C licence registry, MOM Progressive Wage Model floors (PWM 2025: S$3,040 Security Officer basic + allowances = effective ~S$2,200-2,600 per guard/mo cost to customer floor), and competitor websites where public. Most guard-company rates are 'contact for quote' on public sites so estimates flagged as hidden_estimated. Tech/robotics vendors (Oneberry, Otsaw, Ademco) publish SaaS/hardware pricing more openly. Triangulated threat level against Elitez Security's positioning: mid-market SME + events + aviation bundle at S$2,400-3,000/guard/mo with Otsaw robotics augmentation.

categories.tier1_premium: Sovereign-backed or MNC-scale agencies serving government, airports, malls, MNC HQs. S$3,500-4,500/guard/mo. Bureaucratic, slow to quote SMEs.
categories.mid_market_guard: PLRD Grade A/B agencies with 500-2,000 guards, serving MCSTs, commercial buildings, schools. S$2,400-3,200/guard/mo. Direct Elitez Security competitive set.
categories.event_boutique: Specialist crowd-control, F&B venue, concert, conference security. Per-event pricing (S$25-45/guard/hr). Rarely do long-contract work.
categories.tech_ai_robotics: Robotics, video analytics, access control, SOC-as-a-service vendors. Sell hardware + SaaS, partner with guard agencies. Hardware CapEx S$30K-150K per robot; SaaS S$200-800/camera/mo.
categories.facility_mgmt: Integrated FM conglomerates with security bolt-on. Security is 15-25% of bundle; rarely best-in-class but bundled pricing wins procurement.
categories.aviation_specialist: Airport-pass holders with IATA/CAAS clearance. S$3,800-5,200/guard/mo premium for aviation-licensed staff.
categories.diy_alt: Gig-style or direct-hire platforms (Myswak, Staffany, direct MOM hire). Avoids agency margin but shifts compliance burden to end customer.
categories.budget_subcontractor: Small Grade B/C agencies, often subcontractors. S$1,800-2,200/guard/mo. High turnover, basic service only.

competitors.name: Certis Group
competitors.target_market: SG government contracts (ICA, SPF auxiliary), Changi Airport, Temasek-linked portfolio, enterprise malls (CapitaLand, MapleTree)
competitors.sg_pricing_range_published: Not public; GeBIZ awards suggest S$3,800-4,500 per guard/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: Largest auxiliary police force in SG with Mozart AI orchestration platform, armed capability, and integrated cash-in-transit.
strengths: Temasek ownership and government access monopoly
Armed auxiliary police — only 3 agencies authorised in SG
Mozart SOC platform integrates guards + tech + cash
weaknesses: Legacy brand perception, slow to innovate beyond Mozart

competitors.name: Aetos Holdings
competitors.target_market: SG government events (F1, Airshow, NDP), Surbana Jurong properties, ICA border, major transport nodes
competitors.sg_pricing_range_published: Not public; tender data S$3,500-4,200 per guard/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: Event-security specialist with 5,000+ multi-skilled workforce and counter-UAV capability, Surbana Jurong-backed.
strengths: Surbana Jurong parent = infrastructure project pipeline

competitors.name: Prosegur Singapore
competitors.target_market: MNC Singapore HQs, European bank branches, high-value cash clients
competitors.sg_pricing_range_published: Not public; industry benchmark S$3,400-4,000 per guard/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: Global security MNC with iSOC integrated operations centre and dynamic (patrol-based) guarding model.
strengths: Global threat intelligence backbone (150K staff worldwide)
weaknesses: Limited local brand awareness vs Certis/Aetos
Small SG footprint (~500 staff) limits scale deployments

competitors.name: Securitas Singapore
competitors.target_market: Nordic/European MNCs in SG, pharma/semiconductor plants, data centres
competitors.sg_pricing_range_published: Not public; benchmark S$3,500-4,000 per guard/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: Swedish-heritage data-driven security with strong remote-guarding technology and ESG credentials.
strengths: Remote guarding reduces 24/7 cost by ~50% for low-risk sites
weaknesses: Passive local marketing — hard to discover
Thin SG footprint (~400) limits bench for large deployments

competitors.name: G4S Singapore (Allied Universal)
competitors.target_market: MNC clients with global G4S master agreements, embassies, cash-in-transit
competitors.sg_pricing_range_published: Not public; benchmark S$3,500-4,000 per guard/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: World's largest security brand post-Allied Universal merger; global contracts flow to SG ops.
weaknesses: Allied Universal transition still unsettled locally

competitors.name: Soverus
competitors.target_market: Commercial buildings, MCSTs, schools, Secura Group portfolio properties
competitors.sg_pricing_range_published: Not public; tender benchmarks S$2,700-3,100 per guard/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: SGX-listed Secura Group subsidiary combining guards, integrated command centre, and investigation services.

competitors.name: Secura Group
competitors.target_market: SG commercial + industrial, SGX-listed parent of Soverus and related brands
competitors.sg_pricing_range_published: Not public; estimate S$2,700-3,100 per guard/mo
competitors.primary_value_prop: Listed holding co with manpower, security systems, fire-safety arms under one roof.

competitors.name: Ademco Security Group
competitors.target_market: Enterprise clients needing access control + IP CCTV + fire integration, Grade A buildings
